paper_title: Integrative Analysis of Pollen-Specific HRGP Motifs

methods.description: This method describes the initial process of retrieving gene promoters and performing ensemble de novo motif discovery using Emotif-Alpha on pollen-specific HRGP genes against a background of non-pollen HRGP genes.

steps.description: Gene promoters (within 1kb upstream of the translation start site) were retrieved from Ensembl Biomart [21].
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve gene promoter sequences from Ensembl Biomart.
outputs: Promoter sequences for pollen-specific HRGP genes
Promoter sequences for non-pollen HRGP genes

steps.description: Next, we performed ensemble motif discovery using Emotif-Alpha [7] on the 13 pollen-specific HRGP genes against the background of 132 non-pollen HRGP genes. Emotif-Alpha integrated 11 motif discovery tools and led to the identification of 3519 motifs in total.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ensemble de novo motif discovery using Emotif-Alpha.
inputs: Promoter sequences for pollen-specific HRGP genes (foreground)
Promoter sequences for non-pollen HRGP genes (background)
outputs: Set of discovered de novo motifs (3519 total)
Motif occurrence data (locations, frequencies) in input sequences

methods.description: This method applies Filter A criteria to the set of discovered motifs, selecting those highly specific to pollen-specific HRGP genes based on frequency thresholds and conservation, followed by comparison to known motifs.

steps.description: Filter A selected motifs present in 11 (85%) or more of the pollen-specific HRGP genes.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter discovered motifs by foreground frequency threshold.
inputs: Set of discovered de novo motifs (from Ensemble Motif Discovery)
Motif occurrence data in pollen-specific HRGP promoters
outputs: Subset of motifs meeting foreground frequency criteria

steps.description: Filter A selected motifs present in at most 30 (23%) non-pollen HRGP genes.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter motifs by background frequency threshold.
inputs: Motif occurrence data in non-pollen HRGP promoters
outputs: Subset of motifs meeting both foreground and background frequency criteria

steps.description: This yielded 13 motifs, which were also conserved in Arabidopsis lyrata.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter motifs based on conservation in Arabidopsis lyrata.
inputs: Arabidopsis lyrata genomic data or motif conservation data
outputs: Final set of 13 motifs after Filter A criteria

steps.description: Interestingly, we found that 4 out of the 13 motifs matched to known motifs based on analysis using TOMTOM [9] (p-value < 0.001).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare identified motifs to known motif databases using TOMTOM.
inputs: Final set of 13 motifs from step 3
outputs: Motif similarity matches to known motifs

steps.description: The foreground coverage indicates the percentage (and number) of pollen-specific HRGP genes having an identified motif in its promoter region. The background coverage is the percentage (and number) of the non-pollen HRGP genes having that motif in its promoter region. The relative frequency is the foreground coverage divided by the background coverage.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate motif coverage statistics (foreground, background, relative frequency).
inputs: Final set of 13 motifs from step 3
Motif occurrence data in pollen-specific and non-pollen HRGP promoters
outputs: Table or data structure containing foreground coverage, background coverage, and relative frequency for each motif

methods.description: This method applies Filter B criteria, which uses known pollen-expressed transcription factor binding motifs to filter the discovered de novo motifs, followed by filtering for conservation, occurrences, and redundancy.

steps.description: First, 99 motifs were identified, based on the cognate transcription factors expressed (based on Araport11 [5]) in pollen (i.e., log2 gene expression ≥1).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and retrieve list of pollen-expressed transcription factors.
outputs: List of pollen-expressed transcription factor genes

steps.description: First, 99 motifs were identified, based on the cognate transcription factors expressed... and obtained from the plantTFDB [8].
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve known binding motifs for pollen-expressed transcription factors from plantTFDB.
inputs: List of pollen-expressed transcription factor genes (from step 1)
outputs: Set of 99 known binding motifs for pollen-expressed TFs

steps.description: Known motif similarity found that 1341 de novo motifs out of the total 3519 motifs were highly similar to these 99 motifs (p < 0.001).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare de novo motifs to known pollen TF motifs using similarity analysis.
inputs: Set of discovered de novo motifs (3519 total, from Ensemble Motif Discovery)
Set of 99 known binding motifs for pollen-expressed TFs (from step 2)
Motif similarity tool (e.g., TOMTOM or similar)
outputs: Subset of 1341 de novo motifs highly similar to known pollen TF motifs

steps.description: The set of motifs was then filtered by conservation and number of occurrences...
steps.searchable_summary: Filter the subset of similar motifs by conservation and occurrence criteria.
inputs: Subset of 1341 similar de novo motifs (from step 3)
outputs: Subset of motifs meeting conservation and occurrence criteria

steps.searchable_summary: Identify and remove redundant motifs from the filtered set.
outputs: Final set of non-redundant motifs after Filter B criteria

steps.description: The foreground coverage indicates the percentage (and number) of pollen-specific HRGP genes having the identified motif in their promoter regions. The background coverage is the percentage (and number) of the non-pollen HRGP genes having that motif in their promoter regions. The relative frequency is the foreground coverage divided by the background coverage.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate motif coverage statistics (foreground, background, relative frequency).
inputs: Final set of non-redundant motifs from step 5
Motif occurrence data in pollen-specific and non-pollen HRGP promoters
outputs: Table or data structure containing foreground coverage, background coverage, and relative frequency for each motif
